title: eriogonum umbellatum var. lautum
intro: Eriogonum umbellatum var. lautum, also known as Sulfur Buckwheat or Lautum Sagebrush Buckwheat, is a variety of the Eriogonum umbellatum plant, a flowering shrub native to western North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Eriogonum umbellatum var. lautum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water, and it is also sometimes used in tinctures and salves.
